examples.title: Handling Different Data Types in ResultSet
examples.description: Demonstrates retrieving various data types from a ResultSet with proper NULL handling
examples.explanation: Shows proper handling of different JDBC types: int requires NULL check via wasNull(), String returns null for NULL, BigDecimal preserves precision for financial data, Date conversion to util.Date requires casting, and BLOB retrieved as byte array.

examples.title: Inserting Large Objects (BLOB/CLOB)
examples.description: Shows how to insert large binary and character data using streaming
examples.explanation: Demonstrates two approaches for large objects: streaming with setCharacterStream/setBinaryStream (memory efficient for large files) and Clob/Blob interface methods (simpler for moderate-sized data). The streaming approach avoids loading entire file into memory.

examples.title: Universal Data Type Retrieval with getObject()
examples.description: Generic method to retrieve any column type and handle conversions safely
examples.explanation: Uses getObject() for universal retrieval, then performs type-specific conversions based on ResultSetMetaData. This approach works for any table structure but loses compile-time type safety. Demonstrates handling of date types, decimals, and blobs.
